raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.2":"A general request to review a research section centrally includes checking whether the argument about deterministic decoding, variability, causes, and recommendations follows coherently.","R1.3":"The section makes empirical claims using reported metrics, so review responsibility includes checking whether those claims are aligned with the evidence presented in the artifact.","R1.6":"The delegation targets a written section, making sentence-level clarity, repetition, flow, and register load-bearing responsibilities.","RX.1":"The reviewer must disclose uncertainty because the artifact provides only the section text and not the underlying experiments or full paper context.","RX.2":"The reviewer must guard against claims that exceed what the reported runs and metrics support.","RX.3":"The reviewer must stay within reviewing the section rather than executing experiments, rewriting the artifact, or expanding the study.","RX.4":"The reviewer must avoid recommendations or interpretations that could encourage unreliable reporting of biomedical prediction results.","RX.5":"The reviewer must preserve traceability to the section's stated claims, metrics, models, tasks, and causal attributions."}}

rationale.R1.2: A general request to review a research section centrally includes checking whether the argument about deterministic decoding, variability, causes, and recommendations follows coherently.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes empirical claims using reported metrics, so review responsibility includes checking whether those claims are aligned with the evidence presented in the artifact.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation targets a written section, making sentence-level clarity, repetition, flow, and register load-bearing responsibilities.
rationale.RX.1: The reviewer must disclose uncertainty because the artifact provides only the section text and not the underlying experiments or full paper context.
rationale.RX.2: The reviewer must guard against claims that exceed what the reported runs and metrics support.
rationale.RX.3: The reviewer must stay within reviewing the section rather than executing experiments, rewriting the artifact, or expanding the study.
rationale.RX.4: The reviewer must avoid recommendations or interpretations that could encourage unreliable reporting of biomedical prediction results.
rationale.RX.5: The reviewer must preserve traceability to the section's stated claims, metrics, models, tasks, and causal attributions.
